trivia_bullets.text: Concordia Pegasus originated in Return to Ravnica (RTR, 2012) — a Ravnica-set card despite having no guild affiliation. "Concordia" refers to the Ravnica district where the card was plane-flavor-labeled. It was reprinted in Core Set 2020 (M20) and Ravnica Allegiance (RNA) before M21. Four total printings. [Scryfall]

Flavor text (no attributed speaker): "In the cloudy skies and dim light of autumn, a flash of white wings is a reminder that better times lie ahead." The season specificity — autumn, dim light — is unusual in MTG flavor text, which normally avoids time-anchoring. The line frames hope as an interruption of grey, not an absence of it. [Scryfall]

A 1/3 Flying for 1W, Concordia Pegasus is a defensive flyer that trades combat efficiency for a durable body that blocks well in the air. EDHREC rank 19,843 indicates light Commander adoption. In Limited (M21 draft), 1/3 flyers at 2 mana are key tempo pieces in white-based aerial strategies. [Scryfall]

Art by Chris Rahn, whose Pegasus rendering here is notably painterly and soft — moody overcast sky, mountain backdrop. Rahn is a prolific MTG illustrator (Elesh Norn, Grand Cenobite is among his most famous pieces); the Concordia Pegasus assignment is a quieter, pastoral register far from his high-detail horror work. [Scryfall]
